arts.ttl: 'Let's be honest' - Mark Robins makes admission after Coventry City go close against 'relieved' Tottenham - CoventryLive
arts.sum: The Sky Blues took a deserved second half lead through Brandon Thomas-Asante but passed up chances to kill the game before Ange Postecoglou sent on his big guns, including Cov Kid James Maddison, Dejan Kulusevski and Son, before Djed Spence and Brennan Johnson turned the game in Spurs' favour with goals in the 88th and 92nd minutes. For the second game running, however, Robins was left to rue missed opportunities in front of goal.

arts.ttl: Johnson saves Postecoglou, Moore ignored and things spotted in Coventry v Spurs
arts.sum: Brennan Johnson was the hero for Tottenham as they survived a major scare to beat Coventry City 2-1 in the third round of the Carabao Cup. Following last Sunday's North London Derby defeat, Ange Postecoglou made a total of eight changes to his Spurs side as players such as Fraser Forster, Archie Gray and Lucas Bergvall came into the side. Heading to Coventry with the chance to really kickstart their season and get things up and running in those attacking areas, Spurs actually finished the first half with no shots on goal.

arts.ttl: Postecoglou press conference LIVE - Spurs boss on Djed Spence and Odobert injury
arts.sum: Coventry caused them problems and scored after the hour mark when Norman Bassette got down the right and whipped the ball across to Brandon Thomas-Asante to slot home. The game against Mark Robins' Championship side was always going to be a potential banana skin for Tottenham in the days after Postecoglou made it clear that he is aiming to continue his career theme of second season silverware.

arts.ttl: Spurs player ratings vs Coventry - Spence, Johnson and Kulusevski save Spurs
arts.sum: Made an absolute mess of coming out to claim a ball in the second half, collided with Dragusin and was fortunate that Davies made a sliding block in front of the Spurs goal. Coventry caused them problems and scored after the hour mark when Norman Bassette got down the right and whipped the ball across to Brandon Thomas-Asante to slot home.

arts.ttl: Ange Postecoglou says ‘spirit and character’ got Tottenham past Coventry
arts.sum: Spurs were minutes away from a second consecutive early Carabao Cup exit under Postecoglou.

arts.ttl: Ange Postecoglou sees his blushes spared as Tottenham's late, late show sees off Coventry
arts.sum: Tottenham boss Ange Postecoglou guaranteed a trophy this season after defeat in Sunday's North London derby, but his side were fortunate to get past Coventry City in the third round of the Carabao Cup

arts.ttl: Highlights: Tottenham leave it late to see off Coventry
arts.sum: Highlights of the Carabao Cup third round tie between Coventry City and Tottenham Hotspur.

arts.ttl: Spurs late show sinks Coventry to keep four trophies available to Ange
arts.sum: Tottenham's late turnaround secured a 2-1 win over Championship side Coventry to progress to the Carabao Cup fourth round and keep four trophies available to Ange Postecoglou in his pursuit of second-season silverware.

arts.ttl: Coventry 1-2 Tottenham: Highlights, man of the match, stats as Spurs win late
arts.sum: Trophy-focused Ange Postecoglou was spared embarrassment on Wednesday as Tottenham scored two late goals to come from behind and beat Coventry City in the EFL Cup.

arts.ttl: Andy Turner's Coventry City player ratings after scintillating Sky Blues go agonisingly close to an upset - Andy Turner
arts.sum: Substitutes Ellis Simms and Ephron Mason-Clark went agonisingly close to extending the lead as City went searching for a second goal to put the tie beyond doubt, while Ben Wilson pulled off a brilliant save to deny Kulusevski at the other end. Bassette then played a square ball to Thomas-Asante in the 63rd minute and the striker hit a clean shot past Forster to give Coventry a deserved lead.
